title: MaraDöner Announces Nürburgring 24 Hours Drive; Väisänen and Thompson Rumoured to Join
meta: Published: 26 March 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Michiii MaraDöner will drive a Porsche 911 GT3 R at the Nürburgring 24 Hours.
body:
LONDON — Michiii MaraDöner has confirmed he will compete in the 2026 Nürburgring 24 Hours, driving a Porsche 911 GT3 R for a privateer team in a one‑off appearance. The announcement, made via social media on Thursday, comes as the Formula 1 paddock enjoys a rare weekend off between Shanghai and Suzuka.

“I hereby announce that I will be driving in the Nürburgring 24 Hours this year,” the Alpine driver wrote. “I am delighted for that opportunity and I am excited to drive these cars.”

MaraDöner, 18, has been one of the breakout rookies of the 2026 F1CC season, scoring a podium in a chaotic wet Australian Grand Prix and adding a sprint podium in China. His aggressive style and outspoken personality have made him a fan favourite. The Nürburgring appearance will be his first competitive outing in GT machinery.

The privateer Porsche squad has not been named, but sources close to the team confirm the entry is for the Nürburgring 24 Hours only. The race, scheduled for 20–21 June, attracts a mix of factory‑backed efforts and private entries, and the addition of an active Formula 1 driver is a significant coup.

Väisänen and Thompson Linked

Paddock speculation, unconfirmed by any of the parties involved, suggests MaraDöner will not be the only F1 name on the grid. Rumours circulating in the endurance racing community indicate that Ferrari’s Eetu Väisänen — currently leading the F1CC championship with two wins from two races — and Mercedes junior Alex Thompson, who drives for Trident in Formula 2, could also join the Porsche entry.

The speculation intensified after Väisänen and MaraDöner were seen in conversation in the VIP paddock of the FIA World Endurance Championship prologue in Qatar on 23 March. The two drivers, who are not known to have a close personal relationship, spent several minutes talking near the Ferrari and Alpine garages. Neither has commented publicly on the nature of their discussion.

Väisänen’s potential involvement is further fuelled by his existing connection to Thompson. The Finn has acted as an informal mentor to the Mercedes‑backed Thompson during his GT3 Spring Challenge campaign this season, in which Thompson has taken two victories at Auto Club Speedway. Thompson has spoken previously of Väisänen’s advice on tyre management and racecraft in endurance conditions. “Eetu has been incredibly generous with his time,” Thompson said earlier this month. “He’s one of the smartest drivers I’ve ever spoken to. Any advice he gives, I take seriously.”

One‑Off Event, No Impact on F1 Campaigns

If confirmed, the Nürburgring appearance would be a one‑off for all three drivers. The race falls during a break in the F1CC calendar between the Canadian and Austrian Grands Prix, meaning no race weekend conflicts. Teams are often cautious about allowing drivers to compete in external events, but privateer GT outings have become increasingly common for drivers seeking additional track time or personal challenges.

MaraDöner’s Alpine team is aware of his entry and has approved the participation. “We support Michiii’s desire to broaden his racing experience,” an Alpine spokesperson said. “The Nürburgring 24 Hours is a legendary event, and driving a GT car there will only add to his development.”

Ferrari have not commented on whether Väisänen will join the effort, and Mercedes has made no statement regarding Thompson’s potential involvement. Both drivers remain focused on their primary championship campaigns. Väisänen leads the F1CC standings with 50 points. Thompson is currently competing in both F2 and the GT3 Spring Challenge.

Nürburgring’s F1 History

The Nürburgring 24 Hours is run on the combined Nordschleife and Grand Prix circuit, a 25.9‑km layout that is widely regarded as the most demanding endurance race in the world. Formula 1 drivers have occasionally taken part, most notably Nick Heidfeld and Nico Hülkenberg, the latter winning in 2015 alongside future F1 driver Nick Tandy. The event’s combination of day and night running, changeable weather, and heavy traffic from over 100 cars makes it a unique challenge.

What it means: MaraDöner’s entry is confirmed. The potential addition of Väisänen and Thompson — both unverified — would create an unprecedented gathering of current F1 and F2 talent at a single GT event. Further announcements are expected as the event approaches.

Last updated: 22:15 BST, 26 March 2026
